model.signal:
<script type="module">
  import { boot } from 'galath/boot';
  await boot({
    source: document.getElementById('galath-source').textContent,
    mount: document.getElementById('mount'),
  });
</script>

x-chapter-heading.title: Welcome to Galath
x-chapter-heading.subtitle: A stronger XML language for declarative web apps. Read top-to-bottom; every chapter is runnable.

div.p: Galath is an XML-based programming language for the web. It blends ideas from XForms (instance trees + path bindings), XAML (templates and attached behaviors), XUL (commandsets), FXML (named controller actions), and modern reactive frameworks (signals, scoped lifetimes, surgical DOM updates).
The result is a small, readable language where most of an app is structure and data. JavaScript is reachable as an escape hatch; you rarely need it.

ul.li: Trees are the right data structure for UI - your view IS a tree.
Tooling already exists: parsers, validators, editors, syntax highlighting.
Content and code coexist: a chapter of prose and a working component live in the same file.
Diffs read cleanly: structural changes look structural in source control.

div.p: No build step. No bundler. Just a browser, an import map, and your XML.
Each chapter introduces one feature, shows it running, and includes the source so you can copy it out and play. Read in order; each chapter builds on the previous one.

ol.li: Signals - the reactive cell that powers everything else.
Instance Tree - state as XML you can navigate with paths.
XML Events - listening to data-tree mutations.
Components - custom elements with props, lifecycle, scoped style.
Imports - splitting an app across files like ES modules.
